name: ionos
description: Inspect IONOS Cloud virtual data centers and servers, and start or stop servers via the Cloud API

envVars.name: IONOS_TOKEN
envVars.description: IONOS Cloud bearer token, generated via the Token Manager in the DCD or GET https://api.ionos.com/auth/v1/tokens/generate; same env var used by ionosctl and the IONOS SDKs

tools.name: List Data Centers
tools.description: List virtual data centers in the account; each data center contains servers, volumes, and networks

depth.description: Detail level 0-10; higher values expand nested resources in the response

limit.description: Maximum number of elements to return

historicalSummary.omitted: entity sub-collections, hrefs, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: List Servers
tools.description: List servers inside a virtual data center

depth.description: Detail level 0-10; use 1+ to include server properties like name, state, cores, and RAM

limit.description: Maximum number of elements to return

historicalSummary.omitted: attached volumes, NICs, hrefs, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Server
tools.description: Get a server in a data center, including name, vmState, cores, RAM, and boot device

depth.description: Detail level 0-10; use 2 to expand attached volumes and NICs

tools.name: Start Server
tools.description: Start (power on) an Enterprise server; CUBE servers cannot be started with this endpoint (they use suspend/resume instead)

tools.name: Stop Server
tools.description: Stop (power off) an Enterprise server; the server is forcefully powered off and billing for compute stops; public IPv4 addresses that are not reserved are returned to the IPv4 pool (reserved IPs are kept). CUBE servers cannot be stopped with this endpoint (they use suspend/resume instead)

tools.name: Reboot Server
tools.description: Force a hard reboot of a server (equivalent to powering it down and back on) — recovers hung servers without a separate stop/start cycle; not a graceful OS reboot

tools.name: Get Request Status
tools.description: Poll the status of an asynchronous write operation (202 Accepted responses) until it reports DONE or FAILED; the request ID comes from the Location response header of the write call

requestId.description: Request UUID from the Location header of an asynchronous write response

prompts.title: Data center overview
prompts.prompt: List my IONOS Cloud virtual data centers and the servers inside each, with their power state.

prompts.title: Stop a server
prompts.prompt: Find the IONOS server I name and stop it after confirming with me.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: IONOS Cloud setup

steps.title: Get an IONOS Cloud account
steps.description: Sign up at https://cloud.ionos.com — new accounts typically include trial credit. Log into the Data Center Designer (DCD) at https://dcd.ionos.com.

steps.title: Generate a bearer token
steps.description: In the DCD open Management > Token Manager and generate a token, or call GET https://api.ionos.com/auth/v1/tokens/generate with Basic auth (Base64 of username:password). Tokens are required if 2FA is enabled.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Copy the token into your .env as IONOS_TOKEN.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Data Centers (depth=1), then List Servers for one of the returned data center IDs.

notes: All Cloud API v6 paths live under https://api.ionos.com/cloudapi/v6
The depth query parameter (0-10) controls how deeply nested resources are expanded; depth=0 returns only IDs
Stopping a server is a forceful power-off; shut down the OS first for clean stops
Write requests return 202 Accepted and complete asynchronously — check the server's vmState afterwards
